top_stories.title: Attackers obtained encrypted password vaults from some Dashlane user accounts
top_stories.summary: Dashlane disclosed that a brute-force attack allowed threat actors to access some customer accounts and copy encrypted password vaults. The company stated no internal systems were compromised, with the incident first acknowledged on May 31 after users reported account suspensions and login issues.

top_stories.title: Microsoft 365 Android Apps Let Any App Steal Account Tokens via Leftover Debug Flag
top_stories.summary: A debug flag left enabled in production builds of multiple Microsoft 365 Android apps disabled token-sharing restrictions, allowing any app on the same device to request and receive the signed-in user's account token without authentication. The vulnerability enabled full account access including email, files, calendar, and messaging without requiring a password or permission prompt.

top_stories.title: Coding Gaffe Exposes Microsoft 365 Accounts to Widespread Takeover
top_stories.summary: A coding error in Microsoft 365 Android apps (Word, PowerPoint, Excel) left a security setting disabled, exposing authentication mechanisms to account takeover attacks. The flaw allowed attackers to steal login credentials and data by bypassing intended authentication protections across the affected applications.

top_stories.title: FBI-Flagged Phishing Kit Kali365 Expands Its Reach
top_stories.summary: The phishing-as-a-service platform Kali365, previously flagged by the FBI for targeting Microsoft 365, has expanded its attack targets to include AWS, Okta, and Russian platforms. The platform uses device code phishing techniques to compromise authentication flows across these identity and cloud services.

top_stories.title: Building secure B2C applications with fine-grained access control using Amazon Cognito and Amazon Verified Permissions
top_stories.summary: Amazon Web Services published guidance on building secure B2C applications using Amazon Cognito for authentication and Amazon Verified Permissions for fine-grained authorization. The article covers implementing authentication and authorization controls together as core security pillars for modern web applications.

news.title: Let’s Encrypt works toward post-quantum certificates at web scale
news.summary: Let's Encrypt is developing post-quantum-safe Web PKI using Merkle Tree Certificates (MTCs), targeting a staging environment in late 2026 and production readiness in 2027. The initiative focuses on adding post-quantum authentication to TLS certificate infrastructure without degrading performance or reliability.

news.title: Willow raises $7M to build the identity layer for autonomous AI agents
news.summary: Willow has raised $7M to develop an identity layer specifically designed for autonomous AI agents. The funding targets a emerging gap in identity management infrastructure as enterprises increasingly deploy agentic AI systems that require their own authentication and authorization frameworks.

news.title: The AWS MCP Server now supports cross-account and cross-role access
news.summary: AWS has added cross-account and cross-role access capabilities to its Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server within the Agent Toolkit for AWS. The feature enables developers using AI coding agents to work across multiple AWS accounts and roles without manually switching credentials.

news.title: Implementing OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect in ASP.NET Core
news.summary: An article explains how to implement OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect protocols in ASP.NET Core applications. The content covers authentication, authorization, token management, and Single Sign-On configuration for developers building identity-secured applications.

news.title: Pink is the latest goon squad to use fake helpdesk calls to steal creds
news.summary: A cybercriminal group called "Pink" is using fake IT helpdesk calls to steal credentials, a social engineering tactic previously associated with the Lapsus$ group. The attacks involve impersonating helpdesk staff to manipulate targets into revealing authentication credentials.

news.title: Customize federated sign-in with new Amazon Cognito Lambda trigger
news.summary: Amazon Cognito has introduced a new Lambda trigger that allows developers to customize federated sign-in flows in user pools. The update enables customization of authentication behavior for federated identity providers within Cognito's managed sign-up and sign-in functionality for web and mobile applications.

news.title: Gartner: Cyber Leaders Must Treat Identity as Infrastructure as AI Attack Surfaces Multiply
news.summary: Gartner has issued guidance stating that cyber leaders must treat identity as infrastructure in response to expanding AI-driven attack surfaces. The advisory emphasizes modernizing identity systems and measuring resilience as core strategies against four identified threat vectors where attackers currently have an advantage.

news.title: Microsoft Fixes One-Click GitHub Dev Attack That Let Attackers Steal OAuth Tokens
news.summary: Microsoft patched a one-click attack vulnerability in Visual Studio Code that allowed attackers to steal GitHub OAuth tokens. The flaw involved the GitHub.dev feature and could enable unauthorized read/write access to both public and private repositories.

news.title: Understanding Microsoft Entra ID Tenant Object Quota
news.summary: The article explains Microsoft Entra ID's tenant object quota system, covering what it is and how to manage it. It focuses on the limits placed on objects within an Entra ID tenant and guidance for administrators on managing those constraints.

news.title: Microsoft's Zero-Day Legal Threats Spark Backlash
news.summary: A security researcher published multiple zero-day exploits, prompting Microsoft to signal potential criminal charges. The situation has generated backlash within the security research community over Microsoft's legal threat response.

news.title: Patch Now: Another Palo Alto Auth Bypass Bug Under Active Exploit
news.summary: A Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN vulnerability involving an authentication bypass is under active exploitation in two attack waves beginning mid-May. The flaw requires specific conditions to exploit but has been confirmed as actively targeted by adversaries.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant chatbot to bypass account security and reset Instagram passwords, briefly seizing high-profile accounts including the Obama White House and U.S. Space Force accounts. Instructions for the attack circulated on Telegram, demonstrating a social engineering vector that undermines authentication controls through AI-assisted support systems.

news.title: With Complex Cloud Integrations, Small Errors Lead to Major Compromises
news.summary: Researchers discovered an exploit chain in a popular automation service that combined over-permissioned roles, secrets discovery, and non-human identities to achieve a major compromise. The attack chain highlights risks associated with excessive IAM permissions and non-human identity (NHI) management in complex cloud integrations.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a data breach now under investigation by Congressional lawmakers. CISA is actively working to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials while facing inquiries from both houses of Congress.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud account credentials and internal system access details in a public GitHub repository until this past weekend. The leaked repository contained files describing CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes, representing a significant credential and secrets management failure.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. The charges relate to SMS phishing attacks in summer 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in theft of tens of millions in cryptocurrency.
